text: Robotic Preperitoneal Right Flank Hernia Repair Our patient is a forty eight year old male status post right partial nephrectomy with resection of the right eleventh rib. Past medical history is significant for kidney cancer and hypertension. Preoperative CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis reveals a large right flank hernia. As you can see, the external oblique muscle is diastatic while the internal and transversus abdominis muscles are completely disrupted, allowing for partial herniation of the right lobe of the liver as well as the right kidney. These are the preoperative coronal cuts.

The patient is positioned in the left lateral decubitus position. A supraumbilical 12mm trocar is inserted for intraperitoneal access, revealing dense adhesions from the right rectus muscle to the right flank. An 8mm subxiphoid trocar is placed, and the robot is docked over the right shoulder. After adhesiolysis of the anterior abdominal wall, an 8mm trocar is inserted in the right lower quadrant, followed by a 12mm accessory trocar in the right mid quadrant for full visualization of the posterior hernia defect. Single arm lysis clears the anterior abdominal wall, allowing accessory ports for further adhesiolysis. The mesentery of the right colon and kidney are reduced from the hernia defect, achieving complete reduction of hernia contents.

The surgeon incises the peritoneum and creates a preperitoneal flap proximal to the medial aspect of the hernia defect. The hernia sac is reduced, revealing the posterior aspect of the defect, which is then closed primarily using OV-lock suture.

The surgeon reduces the pneumoperitoneum to eight millimeters of mercury and completes the re-approximation of the transversus and internal oblique muscles at the posterior aspect of the hernia defect. A 20x25 cm sheet of physio mesh is placed over the repair and secured to the abdominal wall using secure strap tackers. The peritoneal flap is re-approximated with tackers, ensuring no mesh is exposed.

The post-operative CT scan reveals an anterior seroma located behind the external oblique muscle and a pre-peritoneal seroma. The musculature remains approximated. Coronal cuts are displayed, with the lower left side showing the preoperative CT scan.
